walkthrough.title: Lay the Foundation
walkthrough.text: Time to build a skyscraper! Grab the three game boards and place them in a row in the center of the table with the round yellow build points facing up. These boards are the ground floor of your building. You can arrange them in any order you like.

walkthrough.title: Deal the Floor Cards
walkthrough.text: Shuffle all thirty floor cards. Deal three cards to each player -- these go in your hand and are kept secret. Then place three more floor cards face up on the table where everyone can see them. Stack the remaining floor cards face down nearby as a draw pile.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Walls and Extras
walkthrough.text: Put the twenty-four tall walls and twenty-four short walls in a supply pile everyone can reach. Set the three dice (red, blue, and light blue), the four spider monkeys, and the superhero medal nearby. You will need all of these during the game.

walkthrough.title: Pick Your Superhero
walkthrough.text: Each player chooses one of the four superhero figures: Rhino Hero, Giraffe Boy, Big E., or Batguin. Place your chosen hero in front of you. Put any unchosen superheroes back in the box. The best climber at the table gets to go first, and play continues clockwise!

summary.title: Lay the Foundation
bullets: Place 3 game boards in a row, yellow build points face up
Arrange in any order in center of table

summary.title: Deal the Floor Cards
bullets: Shuffle 30 floor cards, deal 3 to each player (secret)
Place 3 face up on table, rest become draw pile

summary.title: Prepare the Walls and Extras
bullets: 24 tall walls + 24 short walls in shared supply
Set out 3 dice, 4 spider monkeys, superhero medal

summary.title: Pick Your Superhero
bullets: Each player picks: Rhino Hero, Giraffe Boy, Big E., or Batguin
Unused heroes go back in the box
Best climber goes first, play clockwise

walkthrough.title: Your Turn at a Glance
walkthrough.text: Every turn follows six steps in order: Build a floor, check for a spider monkey, climb the skyscraper, check for a super battle, check for the superhero medal, and finally draw a new floor card. Do not worry -- each step is quick and fun. Let's walk through them one by one!

walkthrough.title: Step 1 -- Build!
walkthrough.text: Pick one floor card from your hand. The symbols on it show which walls you need: a short symbol means a short wall, and a tall symbol means a tall wall. Place the required walls on the building, then balance your floor card horizontally on top. On the game boards, walls must touch a yellow build point (one wall per point). On upper floors, walls can go anywhere. Floors can be rotated any direction and may overlap other floors slightly, but a floor cannot rest on another floor as a support.

walkthrough.title: Step 2 -- Spider Monkey Attack!
walkthrough.text: Does the floor card you just placed have a spider monkey symbol on it? If yes, grab a spider monkey from the supply and carefully hang it on that floor by its tail or hand. If it falls, keep trying until it stays put. If the supply is empty, take a spider monkey that is already hanging somewhere on the building and rehang it on the new floor. Those sneaky monkeys love causing trouble!

walkthrough.title: Step 3 -- Climb the Skyscraper!
walkthrough.text: Roll the light blue die. The number tells you how many levels your superhero climbs up. If you roll a minus one, your hero moves down one level instead. Levels are counted by looking at the building from the side -- all floors at the same height are one level. If your hero reaches the top, stop there even if you have movement left. If your hero is already on the ground and you roll minus one, your hero stays put.

walkthrough.title: Step 4 -- Super Battle!
walkthrough.text: Did your hero land on a level where another superhero is already standing? Time for a battle! You are the attacker and get the red die. The defender gets the blue die. Both players roll at the same time. The higher number wins and stays; the loser moves down one level. If the loser lands on yet another hero, another battle happens immediately! The newcomer is always the attacker.

walkthrough.title: Step 5 -- Superhero Medal Check
walkthrough.text: At the end of your turn, check: is your superhero the highest one on the building? If so, take the superhero medal! If someone else already has it, they hand it over to you. The medal is important because it determines the winner if the building collapses.

walkthrough.title: Step 6 -- Draw a Floor Card
walkthrough.text: Draw one floor card to bring your hand back up to three. You can draw from the face-down pile or choose one of the three face-up cards. If you take a face-up card, immediately replace it with a new card from the draw pile. Then the next player takes their turn!

walkthrough.title: How the Game Ends
walkthrough.text: The game ends one of two ways. If someone causes the building to partially or completely collapse, the player holding the superhero medal wins -- unless the medal holder is the one who knocked it down, in which case everyone else wins together! Alternatively, if all floor cards run out and no one can play, the player with the medal wins. If no one has the medal at the end, everybody loses and you should start a new game right away!

summary.title: Your Turn at a Glance
bullets: 6 steps: Build, Spider Monkey, Climb, Battle, Medal, Draw
Always follow this order each turn

summary.title: Step 1 -- Build!
bullets: Play 1 floor card from hand, place required walls first
Game board: walls must touch yellow build points (1 per point)
Upper floors: walls go anywhere, floors can rotate freely
Floors may overlap slightly but cannot rest on another floor

summary.title: Step 2 -- Spider Monkey Attack!
bullets: Spider monkey symbol on card? Hang a monkey on that floor
If supply is empty, relocate one from elsewhere on the building

summary.title: Step 3 -- Climb the Skyscraper!
bullets: Roll light blue die for movement (climb up or -1 down)
All floors at same height = one level
Stop at top even if movement remains; stay put if at bottom and roll -1

summary.title: Step 4 -- Super Battle!
bullets: Land on a level with another hero? Battle!
Attacker = red die, defender = blue die, roll simultaneously
Higher number stays; loser drops one level (chain battles possible)

summary.title: Step 5 -- Superhero Medal Check
bullets: Highest hero on the building takes the medal
Medal determines the winner if building collapses

summary.title: Step 6 -- Draw a Floor Card
bullets: Draw 1 card (face-down pile or face-up display) to refill to 3
Replace any face-up card taken from the display

summary.title: How the Game Ends
bullets: Building collapses: medal holder wins (unless they caused it)
If medal holder knocked it down: all other players win together
No floors left: medal holder wins
No one has medal at end: everyone loses!

walkthrough.title: Welcome to Superhero Training!
walkthrough.text: Let's practice a few turns of Rhino Hero: Super Battle! Imagine a three-player game. You are playing as Rhino Hero. Your friends are playing as Giraffe Boy and Batguin. The three game boards are set up in a row with the yellow build points showing. Everyone has three floor cards, and the walls, dice, spider monkeys, and medal are ready. Time to build!

walkthrough.title: Your First Build
walkthrough.text: You go first! Look at your three floor cards. One shows two short wall symbols, one shows a short and a tall wall symbol, and one shows a single tall wall symbol. Let's start with the card that needs two short walls. Grab two short walls from the supply, place each one on a different yellow build point on the game boards, and carefully balance the floor card on top. Nice and steady!

walkthrough.title: No Spider Monkey This Time
walkthrough.text: Check your newly placed floor card -- does it have a spider monkey symbol? Nope, no monkey symbol this time. Lucky you! Skip straight ahead to step 3: time to roll the light blue die and climb.

walkthrough.title: Climbing Up!
walkthrough.text: Roll the light blue die. You rolled a 2! Your Rhino Hero starts on the ground, so climb up two levels. There is only one level of floors so far (the one you just built), so Rhino Hero stops there at the top. Place your figure on the floor card you just placed. Remember: even if you have leftover movement, you stop at the highest level.

walkthrough.title: Medal Time!
walkthrough.text: No other heroes are on this level, so no battle. Now check for the superhero medal -- your Rhino Hero is the highest figure on the building (you are the only one up there!). Take the superhero medal and keep it in front of you. Finally, draw a floor card from the draw pile or pick one of the face-up cards to get back to three cards in hand.

walkthrough.title: Giraffe Boy's Turn
walkthrough.text: Giraffe Boy's player picks a floor card that needs one tall wall. They place the tall wall on another yellow build point and balance their floor card on top -- creating a second floor at a different height since the tall wall is taller! No spider monkey on this card either. They roll the die and get a 3, climbing all the way to the second level (the taller floor). Giraffe Boy is now higher than Rhino Hero!

walkthrough.title: The Medal Changes Hands
walkthrough.text: Giraffe Boy is now the highest superhero on the building. The Giraffe Boy player takes the superhero medal from you. Oof! Do not worry -- you will have a chance to win it back. They draw a card and their turn is done.

walkthrough.title: Batguin Builds and Gets a Monkey
walkthrough.text: Batguin's player plays a floor card with one short wall symbol and a spider monkey symbol. They place the wall on an upper floor and balance the card on top. Now, because of the spider monkey symbol, they must grab a spider monkey and hang it from the new floor by its tail or hand. The monkey dangles there looking mischievous. Batguin rolls a 1 and climbs up one level.

walkthrough.title: Oops -- A Common Mistake!
walkthrough.text: Wait, the Batguin player tried to place a wall directly on top of another floor card, using it as a base. That is not allowed! Walls on the game boards must touch yellow build points, and on upper levels they stand on placed floor cards -- but a floor card itself cannot be used as structural support for another floor card above it. The walls are what hold the floors up. Let's fix that wall placement.

walkthrough.title: Your Second Turn -- Building Higher
walkthrough.text: It is your turn again. You play a floor card that requires one tall wall and one short wall. You place the tall wall on the highest floor and the short wall on a lower floor. Hmm, that means the floor card will be tilted at an angle. Actually, you must place walls so the floor can sit horizontally on top. Let's rethink and put both walls on the same level so the floor card balances flat.

walkthrough.title: Rolling and Battling!
walkthrough.text: Great build! No spider monkey on this card. Now roll the light blue die -- you got a 3! Climb three levels up from where you are. You land on the same level as Giraffe Boy! That means it is time for a super battle. You are the attacker, so you take the red die. The Giraffe Boy player takes the blue die.

walkthrough.title: Battle Results!
walkthrough.text: Both players roll at the same time. You rolled a 4, and Giraffe Boy rolled a 2. You win! Giraffe Boy must move down one level. You stay where you are and proudly claim the superhero medal since you are now the highest hero. Take that medal back!

walkthrough.title: The Building Wobbles!
walkthrough.text: A few more turns pass. The building is now four levels tall with spider monkeys hanging all over it. On Batguin's turn, they carefully place walls for a new floor. As they set the floor card down... oh no! The building shakes. A spider monkey falls, and then one whole side of the tower collapses! Batguin knocked it over!

walkthrough.title: Who Wins?
walkthrough.text: The building collapsed on Batguin's turn. Check who has the superhero medal -- you do! Since you have the medal and you are not the one who knocked the building over, you win the game! If you had been the one to topple the tower, then Giraffe Boy and Batguin would have won together instead. The medal is everything!

walkthrough.title: Another Mistake to Watch For
walkthrough.text: One more thing to remember: when placing walls on the game board (the ground level), each yellow build point can hold only one wall, and your wall must at least touch the point. Some new players try to put two walls on one build point -- that is not allowed! Also remember you can use both hands for building. Just place your hand cards face down in front of you while you build.

walkthrough.title: You Are Ready to Be a Superhero!
walkthrough.text: Awesome job! You now know how to build floors, hang spider monkeys, climb the skyscraper, battle other heroes, and win the superhero medal. Remember: steady hands win games! Keep your walls straight, place your floors carefully, and do not let those spider monkeys distract you. Now go build the tallest skyscraper ever!

summary.title: You Are Ready to Be a Superhero!

Go build the tallest skyscraper ever!

walkthrough.title: Steady Hands, Happy Skyscraper
walkthrough.text: This is a dexterity game, so take your time! Rushing is the number one cause of towers toppling. Place walls slowly, lower floor cards gently, and breathe. Even pros sometimes get the shakes when the tower is five levels tall. Slow and steady really does win the race here.

walkthrough.title: Choose Your Floor Cards Wisely
walkthrough.text: You always have three floor cards in your hand. Look for cards that are easier to place given the current building situation. A card needing one short wall is generally safer than one needing a tall wall and a short wall. Save tricky cards for when the building is more stable (lower levels) and play simpler ones up high where things wobble more.

walkthrough.title: Think About Wall Placement
walkthrough.text: Where you put your walls matters a lot. Try to place walls close together so your floor card has a stable base. Walls spread far apart make for a wobbly foundation. Also think about future turns -- leave good build points available for later floors. Do not use up all the easy spots early.

walkthrough.title: Spider Monkeys Are Not Just Decoration
walkthrough.text: Those dangling spider monkeys add real weight and wobble to the building. When you have a choice of floor cards, think about whether you want to add a monkey to an already shaky area. Hanging a monkey on a high, unstable floor is risky business. If the supply runs out and you have to relocate a monkey, pick one from a stable lower floor.

walkthrough.title: The Medal Is Your Lifeline
walkthrough.text: Always keep an eye on who holds the superhero medal. If you have it, you win when the building falls (as long as you did not cause it). If someone else has it, you want to climb higher to take it from them. Sometimes it is worth battling even if you might lose, just for a shot at the medal.

walkthrough.title: Watch the Face-Up Card Display
walkthrough.text: When drawing a new floor card at the end of your turn, check the three face-up options. If there is a card with an easy wall configuration (like just one short wall), grab it! Picking from the face-up display gives you information and control, while drawing blind from the pile is a gamble.

walkthrough.title: Use Both Hands!
walkthrough.text: The rules allow you to use both hands while building. Take advantage of this! Place your hand cards face down in front of you, then use one hand to hold a wall steady while the other hand places the floor card. Two-handed building is much more stable than trying to do everything one-handed.

summary.title: Steady Hands, Happy Skyscraper
bullets: Take your time -- rushing causes collapses
Place walls slowly, lower floors gently

summary.title: Choose Your Floor Cards Wisely
bullets: Play simpler cards on higher, wobblier levels
Save complex cards for stable lower levels

summary.title: Think About Wall Placement
bullets: Walls close together = more stable foundation
Leave good build points for future turns

summary.title: Spider Monkeys Are Not Just Decoration
bullets: Monkeys add weight and wobble -- place carefully
Relocating? Pick one from a stable lower floor

summary.title: The Medal Is Your Lifeline
bullets: Medal holder wins when building collapses (if they didn't cause it)
Climb higher and battle to take the medal

summary.title: Watch the Face-Up Card Display
bullets: Pick easy-to-place cards from the face-up display
Face-up = informed choice; draw pile = gamble

summary.title: Use Both Hands!
bullets: Set hand cards face down, build with both hands
One hand holds wall steady, other places the floor

walkthrough.title: Build to Make Life Hard for Others
walkthrough.text: Strategic wall placement is not just about your own stability. If you place walls in awkward positions or use up the best build points, the next player has fewer good options. Leaving only difficult spots forces opponents into risky builds that might topple the tower on their turn, not yours.

walkthrough.title: Timing Your Climbs
walkthrough.text: You do not always want to be the highest hero. If you are in the lead with the medal and the building looks shaky, being at the top is actually great -- you just need to not knock it over yourself. But if someone else has the medal and the building is about to fall, it might not matter how high you climb. Think about whether you need the medal right now or can wait.

walkthrough.title: Battle Math Matters
walkthrough.text: The red (attacker) and blue (defender) dice are not the same! Pay attention to the number distributions on each die. Sometimes attacking is favorable, sometimes defending is. If you can choose whether to land on a contested level, consider whether the attacker die gives you good odds before committing to a battle.

walkthrough.title: The Spider Monkey Gambit
walkthrough.text: Playing a floor card with a spider monkey symbol adds wobble to the tower. If you are holding the medal and want the game to end on someone else's turn, play monkey cards in tricky spots to make the building extra unstable. Just make sure you can survive your own build first! This is a sneaky way to push the game toward a collapse you do not cause.

walkthrough.title: Card Counting and Hand Management
walkthrough.text: There are only thirty floor cards total. As the game progresses, pay attention to what has been played. If you see lots of tall-wall cards already on the building, the remaining cards in the draw pile might skew toward short walls. Also keep an eye on the face-up display to plan future turns. Picking up a great card now can set you up for a smooth turn later.

walkthrough.title: Vary the Base Difficulty
walkthrough.text: For a real challenge, flip the game boards so fewer yellow build points are showing. With only five starting points instead of ten, wall placement on the ground floor becomes extremely tight. This forces everyone to build upward faster and makes the early game just as tense as the late game. Great players welcome fewer build points!

walkthrough.title: The One-Handed Challenge
walkthrough.text: Once your group gets comfortable, try the advanced rule of building with only one hand -- or even only your non-dominant hand! This massively increases the difficulty and makes every wall placement a nail-biter. It also levels the playing field between experienced and newer players, since everyone is struggling equally.

summary.title: Build to Make Life Hard for Others
bullets: Use up good build points to leave opponents awkward spots
Force risky builds that might cause their collapse

summary.title: Timing Your Climbs
bullets: Hold medal + shaky building = good position (just don't knock it)
Sometimes staying lower is smarter than chasing height

summary.title: Battle Math Matters
bullets: Red (attack) and blue (defend) dice have different distributions
Consider odds before choosing to land on a contested level

summary.title: The Spider Monkey Gambit
bullets: Play monkey cards to destabilize the building when you have the medal
Make others collapse the tower on their turn, not yours

summary.title: Card Counting and Hand Management
bullets: Track what wall types have been played to predict remaining cards
Pick great face-up cards early to plan future turns

summary.title: Vary the Base Difficulty
bullets: Flip boards for fewer build points (5 instead of 10)
Tight ground floor forces faster vertical building

summary.title: The One-Handed Challenge
bullets: Build with one hand or non-dominant hand for extra difficulty
Levels the playing field for mixed-experience groups

entries.definition: One of the three cardboard base pieces that form the ground floor of the skyscraper. Each has yellow build points on one side and red build points on the other. Placed in a row at the start of the game.
A round colored dot on the game boards. Walls placed on the ground floor must touch a build point. Only one wall per build point. Yellow side has more points (easier); red side has fewer (harder).

A card that acts as a floor of the skyscraper. Shows symbols indicating which walls are needed (short, tall, or both). Some also have a spider monkey symbol. Players hold 3 in hand and play 1 per turn.
The shorter of the two wall types. Used when a floor card shows the short wall symbol. There are 24 short walls in the game.
The taller of the two wall types. Creates a higher level when used. There are 24 tall walls in the game. Floors built on tall walls are higher and often wobblier.
A player's character piece. The four superheroes are Rhino Hero, Giraffe Boy, Big E., and Batguin. Each player controls one figure that climbs the skyscraper.
A small figure that hangs from floor cards by its tail or hand. Placed when a floor card has a spider monkey symbol. Adds weight and instability to the building. There are 4 in the game.
A token held by the player whose superhero is currently the highest on the building. Determines the winner when the building collapses. Changes hands throughout the game.
The movement die rolled every turn to determine how many levels your superhero climbs. Can show positive numbers (climb up) or -1 (move down one level).
The attacker's die in a super battle. Given to the player who just arrived on a contested level.
The defender's die in a super battle. Given to the player who was already on the level before the attacker arrived.
A horizontal tier of the skyscraper when viewed from the side. All floors at the same height count as one level, even if they are separate floor cards. Levels determine climbing movement and battles.

A dice duel triggered when two superheroes end up on the same level. The attacker (new arrival) rolls the red die, the defender rolls the blue die simultaneously. Higher roll wins; loser drops one level. Chain battles can occur.
When a battle loser drops to a level with yet another superhero, a new battle immediately occurs. The falling hero is the new attacker. This can chain multiple times.
When any part of the skyscraper falls during a player's turn. Immediately ends the game. The medal holder wins unless they caused the collapse.
The face-down stack of remaining floor cards. Players draw from here or the face-up display at the end of each turn. When it runs out and no one can play, the game ends.
Three floor cards placed face up on the table. Players may draw from these instead of the draw pile. Replaced immediately when taken.
In a super battle, the player whose hero just moved to the contested level. The attacker always uses the red die.
In a super battle, the player whose hero was already on the level. The defender always uses the blue die.
If none of your floor cards can be legally placed, you may skip your build step and trade one, two, or all three cards by placing them at the bottom of the draw pile and drawing replacements.
If spider monkeys accidentally fall off during building, the active player must rehang them somewhere on the skyscraper before continuing their turn.
If your hero is on the first floor above the ground and you roll -1, your hero moves to the ground and you lose the medal. The medal returns to the supply until someone else claims it.
Floor cards must be placed horizontally (flat) on top of walls. Walls must be positioned so the floor balances level. Tilted or angled floors are not allowed.
